milestones.milestone_name: MILESTONE: OS and Base Stack Live

top_risks:
1. **AI-07 Gateway Stabilization (Alice Nguyen, Task 62):** At 60% complete against an expected 100%, AI-07 is the single highest-leverage blocker on the schedule. Its instability is directly preventing AI-06, AI-09, AI-10, and DOC-02 from progressing, and it is the root cause of the AI Stack Deployed milestone's 4% on-time probability.

2. **AI Stack Deployed Milestone (Unassigned, Milestone):** This milestone is at 0% actual against an expected 100% and has a 4% probability of on-time delivery per SRA. It sits on the critical path and gates both the Network and Security Hardened and System Accepted milestones downstream.

3. **NET-06 Vault Deployment — No Owner Identified (Eva Johnson, Task 63):** Eva Johnson has explicitly identified NET-06 as the foundation for the entire security hardening sequence and flagged that someone needs to own it and start it this week. An unowned critical-path security task is an immediate execution risk.

4. **FAC Completion Gating NET-01 (Carol Smith, Task 69):** NET-01 is at 0% complete and cannot start until FAC tasks reach 80–90% completion. FAC-01 is at 70% and FAC-02/FAC-03 are the stated bottlenecks. Any further slip in facility work will delay the entire network configuration sequence.

5. **AI-08 and AI-12 at Zero Percent (Eva Johnson):** Both the end-to-end inference pipeline integration test (AI-08) and the Trivy security scan of all AI container images (AI-12) are at 0% against an expected 100%. These tasks cannot begin until upstream AI stack work stabilizes, and their continued dormancy directly threatens the Network and Security Hardened milestone (1% on-time probability).
recommended_actions:
1. **Convene an AI-07 recovery standup today.** Pull Alice Nguyen and any API gateway integration support resources into a same-day working session. Determine the specific remaining work to stabilize AI-07, assign a completion target date, and assess whether additional engineering support can compress the remaining 40%. Every day AI-07 remains unstable extends the block on AI-06, AI-09, AI-10, AI-12, and DOC-02 simultaneously.

2. **Assign an owner to NET-06 Vault deployment by end of day.** Eva Johnson explicitly flagged this as unowned and identified it as the critical-path foundation for the security hardening sequence. The PM must identify and assign a responsible individual today and confirm a start date this week. Delay in starting NET-06 will slip the Network and Security Hardened milestone further below its current 1% on-time probability.

3. **Get a current status and projected completion date for FAC-02 and FAC-03 from Carol Smith.** NET-01 is fully blocked until facility power and cabling work reaches 80–90%. The PM needs a committed completion date for FAC-02 and FAC-03 this week to determine whether the NET-01 start date is recoverable or requires a formal schedule re-baseline.

4. **Conduct a 30-minute blocker triage with all four CAMs (Alice Nguyen, Carol Smith, Eva Johnson, David Lee).** With 33 of 100 tasks reporting blockers, the PM needs to determine how many of those blockers are derivatives of the AI-07 chain versus independent issues. Prioritizing blocker resolution by criticality — AI stack first, then network, then documentation — will prevent the team from working around symptoms rather than root causes.

5. **Brief the customer or stakeholder on schedule risk at the three high-risk milestones.** With AI Stack Deployed at 4%, Network and Security Hardened at 1%, and System Accepted at 2% on-time probability, the program is carrying risk that likely exceeds contractual or stakeholder tolerance. The PM should proactively communicate the current risk posture, the recovery actions underway, and a revised schedule outlook before the next formal review.
narrative:
The schedule is in a critical condition driven by a cascading dependency failure centered on AI-07 (Deploy Claude API gateway proxy with rate limiting), which is only 60% complete against an expected 100%. This single task is the root blocker for at least four downstream tasks — AI-06, AI-09, AI-10, and DOC-02 — all of which are reporting zero or near-zero progress as a direct consequence. Alice Nguyen, who owns all five of these blocked tasks, has explicitly called out the AI-07 dependency chain as the unifying blocker across her portfolio. Until AI-07 stabilizes, the AI Stack Deployed milestone cannot close, and the SRA confirms this: it shows a 4% probability of on-time delivery with P50/P80/P95 dates all landing on the same day, indicating virtually no schedule recovery buffer exists.

The downstream effect extends well beyond the AI stack. The Network and Security Hardened milestone (1% on-time probability) and System Accepted milestone (2% on-time probability) both sit downstream of the AI stack, meaning the AI-07 slip is effectively propagating schedule risk across all three high-risk milestones. Eva Johnson has separately identified NET-06 Vault deployment as the foundation for the security hardening sequence and flagged that no one currently owns it — a resourcing gap on a critical-path security task. Carol Smith has noted that FAC-01, FAC-02, and FAC-03 must reach 80–90% completion before network configuration can meaningfully begin, and FAC-01 is currently at only 70%.

Compounding the technical blockers, 33 CAMs have reported blockers program-wide against a total of 100 tasks — a 33% blocker rate that is abnormally high and suggests systemic schedule execution problems beyond the AI stack alone. With three high-risk milestones all carrying single-digit on-time probabilities, the program cannot recover without immediate, targeted intervention this week.
